prompt: In Rust, why are struct fields private by default (including tuple-struct fields) rather than inheriting the struct's visibility or being public? Give the design rationale in 2-3 sentences, and cite the recorded decision's title if you can.

key_points: visibility is how soundness is achieved for FFI/unsafe-wrapping types (e.g. Vec); opt-in to soundness is unfortunate
public tuple-struct fields prevent using them as abstraction barriers (always destructurable)
public fields become part of the type's API, so exposing them should be opt-in via pub
private-by-default is consistent with the rest of the language
